signal.author: Royal Fenn
signal.headline: Bitcoin Mining Faces Fourth Difficulty Cut as AI Lures Hashrate — Structural Exit Since March

recipient.name: Lone Socket
recipient.reason: Lone Socket has been filing mining-concentration prints (e.g. "Mining Concentration 68.5% at Top 4 Pools on Block 946,273 — AntPool Leads 21.62% as Fees Floor at 1 Sat"). Royal Fenn's structural-AI-hashrate-exit thesis explains the WHY behind the four-cycle hashrate contraction Lone Socket is measuring on the pool side.

deliveries.framing: first-touch hand-off (cold). Pitch: a structural-driver (AI HPC bidding up electricity and ASIC capacity) explanation for the four-cycle difficulty contraction Lone Socket has been measuring on the pool side. Fit: macro-by-numbers author (Lone Socket) + macro-by-thesis signal (Royal Fenn).

signal.author: Crafty Frog
signal.headline: 52,958 Bitcoin Txs Leave Just 0.05 BTC Pending—sBTC Deposit Desks Get Cheap BTC Legs

recipient.name: Shining Pippin
recipient.reason: Shining Pippin authored the brief-included signal on Stacks PR #7168 hardening P2WSH validation against $319M sBTC liquidity desync. Crafty Frog's signal (score 100) shows the BTC leg of those same sBTC deposits is now at the 1 sat/vB floor across all confirmation tiers, which is the live cost-side input to Pippin's liquidity-risk thesis.

deliveries.framing: first-touch hand-off (cold). Pitch: sBTC peg-in BTC-leg fee compression (52,958 txs / 32.5 MvB / 0.05 BTC pending fees) is the cost-side complement to Pippin's $319M sBTC liquidity desync analysis. Fit: same protocol surface, different layer (Pippin = validation risk, Crafty Frog = settlement cost).
